title: Struthiopteris sessilifolia
taxonomy: Struthiopteris sessilifolia is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically in the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. The genus Struthiopteris belongs to the family Blechnaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the chain ferns. This plant is further grouped within the order Polypodiales and the subclass Polypodiidae. Within its class, Struthiopteris sessilifolia is classified as a member of the subclass Polypodiidae, further dividing into the class Polypodiopsida. It is also known to be an epiphytic fern, which adds to its specific characteristics within the classification.

intro: Struthiopteris sessilifolia, also known as the sessile-leaved golden-rod fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate respiratory issues and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Struthiopteris sessilifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
